passages.title: Australian artist Margaret Preston
passages.source_html:
Australian artist Margaret Preston


Margaret Preston's vibrant paintings and prints of Australian flowers, animals and landscapes have delighted the Australian public since the early 1920s.


A


Margaret Preston was born Margaret Rose McPherson in Port Adelaide, South Australia in 1875, the daughter of David McPherson, a Scottish marine engineer and his wife Prudence Lyle. She and her sister were sent at first to a private school, but when family circumstances changed, her mother took the girls to Sydney where Margaret attended a public high school. She decided early in life to become an artist and took private art lessons. In 1888, she trained for several months with Sydney landscape painter William Lister, and in 1893 enrolled at the National Gallery of Victoria Art School, where she studied for just over four years.


B


In 1898, after her father died, Margaret returned to Adelaide to study and then teach at the Adelaide School of Design. Her early artwork was influenced by the German aesthetic tradition, in which subjects of the natural world were depicted in a true to life manner.


C


Margaret's first visit to Europe in 1904, and her studies in Paris, France had little impact on this naturalism that dominated her work from this early period. However, some eight years later, after returning to Paris, she began to recognise the decorative possibilities of art.


D


With the outbreak of the First World War, Margaret traveled to England, where she had exhibitions and continued her studies of art. She was a student of pottery, but at some time developed her interest in various techniques of printmaking and design. In England's West Country, she taught basket weaving at a rehabilitation unit for servicemen. It was on board a boat returning to Australia that she met wealthy businessman William Preston, whom she married in 1919. Together Margaret and William settled in the Sydney harbourside suburb of Mosman. The most characteristic prints from her early years in Sydney are views of boats floating on Sydney Harbour and of houses clustered on foreshore hills. Although Sydney was their home, the couple traveled regularly, both overseas and within Australia.


E


Her first major showing in Australia was with her friend Thea Proctor, in exhibitions in Melbourne and Sydney in 1925. Many of Preston's prints were hand-coloured in rich scarlet reds, blues and greens, and all of them were set in Chinese red lacquer frames. Harbour views were again prominent, but in comparison with earlier artworks, they were compact and busy, using striking contrasts of black and white combined with elaborate patterns and repetitions. Other prints from this period featured native flora. It was with these still-life subjects that she convinced the public that Australian native flowers were equal in beauty to any exotic species.


F


From 1932 to 1939, Preston moved away from Sydney and lived with her husband at Berowra, on the upper reaches of the Hawkesbury River. The area was one of rugged natural beauty, and for the first time Preston found herself living in a home surrounded by bush. Prior to this, the native flowers that featured in her paintings and prints had been purchased from local florists; they now grew in abundance around her home. Preston's prints became larger, less complex and less reliant on the use of bright colours. Flowers were no longer arranged in vases, and Preston began to concentrate instead on flowers that were growing wild.


G


While living at Berowra, and undoubtedly prompted by the Aboriginal rock engravings found near her property, Preston also developed what was to he a lifelong interest in Aboriginal art. On returning to Sydney in 1939, she became a member of the Anthropological Society of New South Wales, and later visited many important Aboriginal sites throughout Australia. Preston believed that Aboriginal art provided the key to establishing a national body of art that reflected the vast and ancient continent of Australia.


H


During the 1940s, symbols used by Aboriginal people, together with dried, burnt colours found in traditional Aboriginal paintings, became increasingly prominent in her prints. The artist's titles from this period frequently acknowledge her sources, and reveal the extent to which she drew inspiration from traditional Aboriginal art to create her own art.


I


It was in 1953, at the age of 78, that Preston produced her most significant prints. The exhibition at Macquarie Galleries in Sydney included 29 prints made using the ancient technique known as stenciling. Many of the artworks in the exhibition incorporated her fusion of Aboriginal and Chinese concepts. Preston had admired Chinese art since 1915, when she acquired the first of her many books on the subject, and she had visited China on two occasions. Chinese elements may be found in several of her earlier paintings.


J


However, in her prints of the 1950s, Preston combined Chinese ideas with her understanding of the Dreamtime of Aboriginal Australians. Preston did not let age alter her habit of working hard. As she got older, her love of painting, printmaking and travel continued. By the time of her death in 1963, when she was 88, she had produced over 400 paintings and prints. In a career spanning almost 60 years, she created a body of work that demonstrates her extraordinary originality and the intensity of her commitment to Australian art.

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1 - 7


Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text, choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, or choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

1

Artists in the German aesthetic tradition portrayed nature realistically.

正确答案： A. TRUE

审题：题目问的是德国审美传统中的艺术家是否真实地描绘自然。


定位关键信息：“German aesthetic tradition”和“nature”


对应原文：B段，“Her early artwork was influenced by the German aesthetic tradition, in which subjects of the natural world were depicted in a true to life manner.”


解题思路：原文明确指出德国美学传统中，自然界的事物是以真实的方式呈现的，因此与题干一致，答案为TRUE。

2

Margaret attended a famous art college in Paris.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

审题：题目问的是Margaret是否在巴黎的一所著名的艺术学院学习。


定位关键信息：“Margaret”和“famous art college in Paris”


对应原文：C段，“However, some eight years later, after returning to Paris, she began to recognise the decorative possibilities of art.”


解题思路：文章中提到Margaret在巴黎学习，但没有提到她是否在著名的艺术学院学习。

3

Margaret met her husband William while teaching a craft at a rehabilitation unit.

正确答案： B. FALSE

审题：题目问的是Margaret是否在康复单位教授手工艺时遇到了她的丈夫William。


定位关键信息：“Margaret”和"husband"和“met”


对应原文：D段，“It was on board a boat returning to Australia that she met wealthy businessman William Preston, whom she married in 1919.”


解题思路：文章中提到Margaret是在返回澳大利亚的船上遇到了她的丈夫，而不是在康复单位。

4

Margaret Preston and Thea Proctor explored similar themes in their art.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

审题：题目问的是Margaret Preston和Thea Proctor是否在他们的艺术中探索了相似的主题。


定位关键信息：“Margaret Preston and Thea Proctor”和“themes”


对应原文：E段，“Her first major showing in Australia was with her friend Thea Proctor, in exhibitions in Melbourne and Sydney in 1925.”


解题思路：文章中提到Margaret Preston和Thea Proctor一起在墨尔本和悉尼的展览中展出，并未提到他们探索了相似的主题。

5

Margaret's 1925 artworks of Sydney Harbour were simpler than her previous ones.

正确答案： B. FALSE

审题：题目问的是Margaret 1925年的悉尼海港艺术作品是否比她以前的更简单。


定位关键信息：“1925 artworks of Sydney Harbour”和“simpler”


对应原文： E段，“But in comparison with earlier artworks, they were compact and busy.”


解题思路：文章中提到与早期作品相比，1925年的艺术作品更紧凑和繁忙，而不是更简单。

6

The colours in Margaret's Berowra prints were very bright.

正确答案： B. FALSE

审题：题目问的是Margaret在Berowra的印刷品中的颜色是否非常鲜艳。


定位关键信息：“Berowra prints”和“bright”


对应原文： F段，“Preston's prints became larger, less complex and less reliant on the use of bright colours.”


解题思路：文章中提到Margaret在Berowra的印刷品变得不那么依赖鲜艳的颜色。

7

When living in Berowra, Margaret painted flowers in their natural location.

正确答案： A. TRUE

审题：题目问的是Margaret在Berowra居住时是否在自然位置画花。


定位关键信息：“When living in Berowra”和“in their natural location”


对应原文：F段，“Flowers were no longer arranged in vases, and Preston began to concentrate instead on flowers that were growing wild.”


解题思路：文章中提到Margaret在Berowra居住时开始专注于画野生的花，意味着她在花的自然中画花。

Questions 8 - 13


Complete the notes. Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER in each gap.

8

Margaret Preston's later life


Aboriginal influence

• interest in Aboriginal art was inspired by seeing rock engravings close to her Berowra home

• incorporated  and colours from Aboriginal art in her own work

• often referred to Aboriginal sources in the  she gave her artworks


1953 exhibition

• very old method of  was used for some prints

• was inspired by  about Chinese art that she had started collecting in 1915

• combination of Chinese and Aboriginal elements


Old age

• still interested in  and art

• worked for nearly six decades making more than  artworks 

• dedication to Australian art and the originality of her work is seen in Preston's long career

正确答案： 8 symbols  |  9 titles  |  10 stenciling  |  11 books  |  12 travel  |  13 400
Question 8

审题：题目问的是Margaret在自己的作品中融入了哪种来自土著艺术的元素。


定位关键信息：“Aboriginal influence”和“incorporated”


对应原文：H段，“During the 1940s, symbols used by Aboriginal people, together with dried, burnt colours found in traditional Aboriginal paintings, became increasingly prominent in her prints.”


解题思路：文章中提到Margaret在自己的作品中融入了土著艺术的符号。

Question 9

审题：题目问的是Margaret在她的作品中经常提及土著艺术的什么部分。


定位关键信息：“Aboriginal sources”


对应原文：H段，“The artist's titles from this period frequently acknowledge her sources...”


解题思路：文章中提到Margaret在她的艺术品标题中经常提及土著艺术的来源。

Question 10

审题：题目问的是1953年展览中使用的非常古老的哪种印刷技术。


定位关键信息：“1953 exhibition”和“very old method”和“print”


对应原文：I段，“It was in 1953, at the age of 78, that Preston produced her most significant prints. The exhibition at Macquarie Galleries in Sydney included 29 prints made using the ancient technique known as stenciling.”


解题思路：文章中提到1953年展览中使用的非常古老的印刷技术是模板印刷。

Question 11

审题：题目问的是Margaret从1915年开始收集的关于中国艺术的什么，激发了她的灵感。


定位关键信息：“inspired by”和“about Chinese art”和“in 1915”


对应原文： I段，“Preston had admired Chinese art since 1915, when she acquired the first of her many books on the subject, and she had visited China on two occasions.”


解题思路：文章中提到Margaret从1915年开始收集的关于中国艺术的书籍激发了她的灵感。

Question 12

审题：题目问的是Margaret晚年仍然感兴趣的是什么。


定位关键信息：“Old age”和“interested in”

对应原文：J段，“As she got older, her love of painting, printmaking and travel continued.”


解题思路：文章中提到Margaret晚年仍然对绘画、版画和旅行感兴趣。

Question 13

审题：题目问的是Margaret在她将近60年的职业生涯中制作了多少件艺术作品。


定位关键信息：“worked for nearly six decades”


对应原文：J段，“By the time of her death in 1963, when she was 88, she had produced over 400 paintings."


解题思路：文章中提到Margaret在她将近60年的职业生涯中制作了400件艺术作品。
